channel.title: Photo Feeds
channel.description: Combined feed of recent photo essays from The Atlantic, The Guardian, The Boston Globe, NYT, and Reuters.

item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Caraballeda, Venezuela — People and rescuers search for victims amid debris of a demolished building in La Guaira state after a magnitude 7.2 earthquake struck Venezuela and other regions in the Caribbean. — Photograph: Edilzon Gamez/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A melon-eating contest and a naked bike ride: photos of the weekend
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Marree, Australia — A camel racer competes during the Marree Australasian Camel Cup in the outback town. The Camel Cup is a much-loved event celebrating camel racing, Afghan cameleer heritage, community and culture. Camels were instrumental in helping settlers open the outback of Australia to development until trains took over. — Photograph: William West/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A melon-eating contest and a naked bike ride: photos of the weekend
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Muharraq, Bahrain — Bahrain civil defence and rescue personnel work in a residential building, which according to the Bahraini interior ministry, was hit by an Iranian drone. — Photograph: Bahrain Police Media/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

Bogotá, Colombia — A participant falls during annual Go Skateboarding Day competitions. — Photograph: Iván Valencia/AP

item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kubbar Island, Kuwait — A lesser-crested tern during the annual breeding season, when thousands of seabirds nest on the island. — Photograph: Yasser Al-Zayyat/AFP/Getty Images

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A melon-eating contest and a naked bike ride: photos of the weekend
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Malé Trakany, Slovakia — People cool off in the waters of the Tisza River as an intense heatwave grips the country. Temperatures are expected to peak over Sunday and Monday, with most of Slovakia placed under the highest, red heat alert affecting large parts of the country. — Photograph: Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A melon-eating contest and a naked bike ride: photos of the weekend
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — Members of staff clean the seats on Court 1 before the start of the Wimbledon Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club. — Photograph: Matthias Hangst/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A melon-eating contest and a naked bike ride: photos of the weekend
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza — A displaced Palestinian girl looks through an opening in her family’s tent. The majority of Gaza’s 2.4 million people have been displaced, often multiple times, since Hamas attacked southern Israel on 7 October 2023. — Photograph: Majdi Fathi/NurPhoto/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A melon-eating contest and a naked bike ride: photos of the weekend
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Çanakkale, Turkey — The elite women set off at the start of the 2026 World Triathlon Development Regional Cup Gallipoli. — Photograph: Yagiz Gurtug/NurPhoto/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A melon-eating contest and a naked bike ride: photos of the weekend
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Beadnell Bay, UK — Wind surfers enjoy the hot weather in Northumberland. An amber warning for extreme heat has been extended but a record-breaking hot spell is coming to a close. — Photograph: Owen Humphreys/PA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, England — A person uses a fan to keep cool in the City of London. The UK government is facing increasingly urgent calls for action to protect people against the intensifying effects of the climate crisis, as the highest maximum temperature record for June was broken for the third day in a row. — Photograph: Andy Hall/The Guardian

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Caracas, Venezuela — Thirty-six hours after back-to-back earthquakes devastated Venezuela’s northern coast, there was scant sign of government help reaching many areas, exposing how years of economic misrule and corruption have left authorities woefully unprepared for a disaster on this scale. — Photograph: Manaure Quintero/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Athens, Greece — Tourists are unmoved by the Aphrodite of Syracuse, a statue of the goddess of love and beauty in ancient Greek mythology, at the National Archaeological Museum in Athens. — Photograph: Thanassis Stavrakis/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Paris, France — Women dance with toy water guns during Fête de la Musique in Paris. — Photograph: Sophia Berger/Hans Lucas/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Ibadan, Nigeria — Twin sisters Taiwo and Kehinde Adediran and twin brothers Taiwo and Kehinde Oguntoye prepare for their wedding in Ibadan. The unisex name Taiwo is traditionally given to the first born of twins and Kehinde to the second. — Photograph: Olympia de Maismont/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Nairobi, Kenya — Kenyan police officers arrest protesters near parliament during a demonstration to mark two years since more than 60 people died in anti-government protests that resulted in the storming of the parliament in Nairobi. — Photograph: Luis Tato/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Tirana, Albania — Protesters carry imitation flamingos as they gather in front of the Albanian prime minister’s office to demonstrate against the construction of a luxury resort near a protected natural area on the country’s coast. The demonstrators argue that the estimated $4bn project, led by the US president’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner, and his wife, Ivanka, will harm the environment. In a country with almost no tradition of civic unrest, the protests, leaderless and non-partisan, have caught officials in Tirana and the EU off guard. — Photograph: Adnan Beci/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Pyongyang, North Korea — Participants gather at the youth park open-air theatre in Pyongyang to mark the Day of Struggle Against US Imperialism. — Photograph: Kim Won Jin/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington DC, US — Chipped paint and algae are visible in the Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool after recent renovations after a directive from Donald Trump to paint it blue before the 250th anniversary of US independence. But it has endured problems with algae, peeling paint and a ballooning price tag. — Photograph: Aaron Schwartz/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Stonehenge, England — Visitors during summer solstice observances at Stonehenge. People continued the age-old tradition of gathering at Stonehenge for the summer solstice, when the earth’s north pole has its maximum tilt toward the sun, which occurs at 9.24am on 21 June. — Photograph: Finnbarr Webster/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, England — The prime minister, Keir Starmer, hugs his wife, Victoria, after announcing his resignation as prime minister and leader of the Labour party outside No 10 Downing Street in London. Less than two years after a historic election victory, Starmer had faced calls from his MPs, including privately from cabinet ministers, to set out a timeline for his departure before the next general election after many of them became unnerved by the threat from Nigel Farage’s Reform UK party. — Photograph: Dan Kitwood/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Manchester, England — People struggle in the extreme heat in Manchester. The UK government is facing increasingly urgent calls for action to protect people against the intensifying effects of the climate crisis, as the highest maximum temperature record for June was broken for the third day in a row. — Photograph: Christopher Thomond/The Guardian

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Paris, France — A couple cool off in the Trocadero fountain during a heatwave in Paris. The number of deaths in France linked to the heatwave has climbed to four toddlers and more than 55 drownings, as the brutally hot conditions sweeping Europe were forecast to shift east, stifling 150 million people with 35C (95F) temperatures. — Photograph: Dimitar Dilkoff/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Guadalupe, Mexico — People watch the World Cup match between South Africa and South Korea at the Monterrey stadium in Guadalupe. — Photograph: David Ramos/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Khan Younis, Gaza — Palestinians watch a live broadcast of the World Cup match between Spain and Saudi Arabia on a screen in Khan Younis. A report by the UN independent international commission of inquiry examined violations against Palestinian children since the start of the war in Gaza, and said about 30% of the people killed by Israeli forces have been children. — Photograph: Abdel Kareem Hana/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Nablus, West Bank — An Israeli soldier aims his weapon fitted with a laser sight while patrolling the market in Nablus. In the ‌West Bank, a UN independent international commission found a sharp increase in ​violence by Israeli settlers against Palestinian children, and documented evidence of torture, including sexual and gender-based violence, during mass arrests and detention. — Photograph: Jaafar Ashtiyeh/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Muscat, Oman — Friends take photographs nearby the waters off Oman where oil tankers, carriers, other ships and Omani fishing boats sit anchored in Muscat. Iran has rejected UN-backed plans for the mass evacuation of ships through the strait of Hormuz, creating a new threat to the free passage of commercial ships through the strait. — Photograph: Elke Scholiers/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Catia La Mar, Venezuela — Damaged residential buildings in Catia La Mar. Tommaso Della Longa, a spokesperson for the International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies, said the earthquake came after a host of challenges that Venezuela had already faced, leaving the country struggling to respond to the disaster. — Photograph: Federico Parra/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Caracas, Venezuela — Rescue workers carry a person on a stretcher out of a collapsed building. Countries around the world, including Iran, the US and Cuba, have committed to help with rescue efforts in Venezuela after deadly twin earthquakes hit the country. — Photograph: Juan Barreto/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Guaira, Venezuela — A man reacts amid the rubble of a building in La Guaira after earthquakes hit the country. Volunteer searchers and the relatives of the many missing voiced exasperation and anger at the lack of an official response as they waited for government teams. — Photograph: Gaby Oráa/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Caracas, Venezuela — People run into a street in Caracas after a 7.1-magnitude earthquake struck Venezuela this week, triggering panic in the city. The interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, has vowed to fight to save ‘as many people as possible’ as the official death toll rose to 920, with nearly 3,000 people injured. — Photograph: Federico Parra/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Federico Parra / AFP / Getty — A man carries a mattress past damaged residential buildings following an earthquake in Catia La Mar, La Guaira state, Venezuela, on June 25, 2026. A pair of massive earthquakes struck on June 24, killing at least 164 people and destroying multiple buildings near the capital.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Tiziana FABI / AFP / Getty — A view of iconic drystone dwellings with conical roofs called trulli, in Alberobello, in the southern region of Puglia, Italy, on June 24, 2026. “With the increase in heat waves, traditional solutions (such as trulli) are becoming a valuable reference, capable of inspiring climate-adaptation strategies for contemporary construction as well,” Gerardo Biancofiore, the head of the Puglia branch of the national builders’ association ANCE, said.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Bay Ismoyo / AFP / Getty — People wade near mangrove trees at sunset at Walakiri beach, in Waingapu, East Sumba, East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia, on June 24, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Annice Lyn / Getty — A man cools off in the Warsaw Fountain at the Jardins du Trocadéro in front of the Eiffel Tower during hot weather on June 24, 2026, in Paris, France. Various European governments issued health warnings this week in relation to the high temperatures, which neared 40 degrees Celsius (104 degrees Fahrenheit) in many large cities.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Shawn Goldberg / SOPA Images / LightRocket / Getty — A dog relaxes on grass during a spring visit to Scarborough Bluffs, along Lake Ontario, in Toronto, Ontario, on June 20, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Daniel Mihailescu / AFP / Getty — A stork eats a locust in a wheat field after a harvester cut the crop near Manastirea village, in Romania, on June 19, 2026. Romanian farmers have been forced to start the annual grain harvest weeks earlier than usual because of an intense early-summer heat wave and prolonged drought conditions.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Özgün Tiran / Anadolu / Getty — Sunflowers bloom in a field in Kırklareli, Turkey, on June 24, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Miguel Medina / AFP / Getty — A lavender field in Valensole, Southern France, photographed on June 22, 2026

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Chen Xi / VCG / Getty — An aerial view of the surging Huangguoshu Waterfall, seen after days of continuous rainfall, on June 23, 2026, in Anshun, Guizhou Province, China

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Elisa Schu / DPA / Getty — A view of the Zugspitze and houses in the valley below, taken from the village of Lermoos, Austria, on June 22, 2026

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Du Juanjuan / Xinhua News Agency / Getty — A bank of clouds rises above the skyline of Beijing, China, on June 24, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Mario Tama / Getty — An aerial view of a massive warehouse fire at a cold storage facility as it continued to burn and spread smoke around the city on June 20, 2026, in Los Angeles, California. California Governor Gavin Newsom declared a state of emergency for Los Angeles County in response to the Lineage Logistics warehouse fire, which began on June 17 in the Boyle Heights neighborhood.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Majid Saeedi / Getty — Iranian mourners take part in a traditional ceremony during Tasua commemorations, held one day before Ashura, on June 24, 2026, in Tehran, Iran.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Nhac Nguyen / AFP / Getty — A train travels on the Long Bien Bridge, in Hanoi, Vietnam, on June 24, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Lou Benoist / AFP / Getty — Jean-Pierre David, the French creator of the individual airship Aeroplume, flies inside an airship hangar in Écausseville, France, on June 24, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Mario Tama / Getty — People gather during a media preview of the retired NASA Space Shuttle Endeavour, displayed in a vertical ready-to-launch position, affixed to the giant orange external tank and twin solid rocket boosters, at the California Science Center on June 24, 2026, in Los Angeles, California.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Yang Meiqing / VCG / Getty — Industrial robots work on a production line producing components for electric vehicles on June 22, 2026, in Jinhua, Zhejiang Province, China.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Oliver Bunic / AFP / Getty — Students prepare to take university-entrance exams at the Belgrade Fair, in Belgrade, on June 25, 2026, as Serbia endures a heat wave affecting much of southeastern and central Europe.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Sai Aung Main / AFP / Getty — People take part in a yoga session to celebrate International Yoga Day at the Thuwanna Indoor Stadium, in Yangon, Myanmar, on June 21, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Lokman Vural Elibol / Anadolu / Getty — Norwegian-national-football-team supporters gather on the iconic red steps in Times Square ahead of Monday’s FIFA World Cup match, creating a festive atmosphere with chants and traditional rowing celebrations in New York City, on June 21, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Ulises Ruiz / AFP / Getty — Michel Kuka Mboladinga, a supporter of the team from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, pays tribute to Congo’s late Prime Minister Patrice Lumumba, by remaining motionless ahead of the 2026 World Cup Group K football match between Colombia and Congo, at Guadalajara Stadium in Zapopan, Mexico, on June 23, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Valentine Chapuis / AFP / Getty — Competitors fight in a mixed-martial-arts arena during the Knights of the Cage, a gala of a medieval armored combat, in Onet-le-Château, France, on June 20, 2026. Fighters in armor with shields and swords, or axes, took part in about 20 fights in an MMA cage in Aveyron.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Atta Kenare / AFP / Getty — Iranians walk between sculptures of workers in Tehran on June 22, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Ben Montgomery / Getty — Participants race in a soapbox car depicting King Charles III on a mobility scooter, during the 2026 Red Bull Soapbox Race at Alexandra Palace, on June 20, 2026, in London, England.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Hilmi Tunahan Karakaya / Anadolu / Getty — A ground squirrel, locally known as gelengi, stands in a meadow in Erzurum, Turkey, on June 24, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Ina Fassbender / AFP / Getty — A red-footed seriema is pictured in its enclosure at Dortmund Zoo, in Dortmund, Germany, on June 24, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Yasuyoshi Chiba / AFP / Getty — The face of seven-month-old giant-panda cub Satrio Wiratama, known as Rio, is covered by the paws of his mother, Hu Chun, at the Panda Palace of Taman Safari Indonesia, in Bogor, West Java, Indonesia, on June 20, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Stone Roofs, Football Fans, Soapbox Racers
item.description:
Sebastien Salom-Gomis / AFP / Getty — The mechanical machine “The Guardian of Darkness,” half-woman, half-scorpion, from the Compagnie La Machine, performs during the 19th edition of the Hellfest rock and heavy-metal festival, in Clisson, France, on June 19, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Alpine heat and jubilant Japan fans: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Chamonix, France — Alpinists walk near the Aiguille du Midi during a heatwave, as high temperatures raise concerns about glacier melt. — Photograph: Pierre Albouy/Reuters

item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — Ground staff brave the sun to prepare the courts before next week’s start of the Wimbledon tennis tournament. — Photograph: Henry Nicholls/AFP/Getty Images

item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Tokyo, Japan — Football fans celebrate after Japan’s 1-1 draw against Sweden in their final World Cup group match, securing both teams’ progression to the knockout stage. — Photograph: Philip Fong/AFP/Getty Images

item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Alpine heat and jubilant Japan fans: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Wehrheim, Germany — A one-week-old sparrow chick that fled out of its nest due to the heat is fed with flies by a vet. — Photograph: Michael Probst/AP

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Alpine heat and jubilant Japan fans: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Tainan, Taiwan — Firefighters evacuate residents after heavy overnight rain from the approaching Typhoon Mekkhala caused severe flooding. — Photograph: Yang Sih-Ruei/CNA/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Alpine heat and jubilant Japan fans: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — Hundreds of staff members, contractors, ITER-project associates, interim personnel, and staff from Fusion for Energy and the other members’ domestic agencies gathered on the ITER platform for a group photo in September 2024.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization / Kevin Ballant — Five of the nine vacuum-vessel modules were in place in the tokamak assembly pit as of June 2026. Once all of the modules are in place, sensors and blanket shielding will be added. The plan is to create a donut-shaped chamber that will use the largest magnets in the world, alongside blanket shielding cooled to nearly absolute zero, to contain 840 cubic meters of plasma that will be 10 times hotter than the core of the sun. The ultimate goal is to create more energy output than input, and to use that to generate electricity in future projects.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — A dense forest of attachments, seen on the inside of the ITER vacuum vessel. Tiny “bosses” for diagnostics are seen here amid the much larger support pads (yellow) for the blanket shield blocks.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
CFoto / Future Publishing / Getty — At the 2026 China International Nuclear Industry Exhibition in Beijing, visitors learn about the ITER reactor while looking at a scale model of the facility, on April 22, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — Standing 4 meters tall, with an arm that extends up to 5 meters, “Godzilla” is a platform for developing and testing the tools and technologies that will be used by robots to install components inside the completed vacuum vessel, photographed in March 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — Workers stand on the cryostat base at the bottom of the tokamak assembly pit on December 17, 2025.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — A team from Italy uses muscle power for the last stage of inserting a gravity support under a vacuum-vessel sector in the tokamak pit in March 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — This insulated 11-by-22-meter lid is lifted by a gantry crane, on its way to close the cryostat of the ITER magnet cold-test facility in April 2026. The cold-test facility will allow massive magnets to be tested at temperatures of about 4 Kelvin (a few degrees above absolute zero) before they are installed in the tokamak.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
Gao Jing / Xinhua / Getty — A vacuum-vessel sector sits on its side in a preassembly hall in Saint-Paul-lès-Durance on November 23, 2023.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — The fifth sector module nears the end of its 12-hour journey from the tooling area, and is lowered by a gantry crane into its final landing spot in the tokamak pit in May 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — One by one, supports for magnetic sensors are welded to the vacuum vessel’s inner surface in February 2026. There are approximately 2,000 such supports per sector.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
Gilles Bader / Le Pictorium / Reuters — A view from inside the heart of the Tokamak reactor, showing mounting points for diagnostic sensors and interior blanket shielding, seen on November 17, 2025

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — A worker from the CNPE Consortium engineering group rappels down one of the massive vacuum-vessel sectors in the pit, past interior staging that has been installed to give teams access to every corner.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — A vacuum-vessel module (top) was installed in the ITER tokamak pit during an overnight operation from May 26 to 27, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
.© ITER Organization — To the right, a 330-ton toroidal field coil (a powerful superconducting magnet) is being tilted up, raised from horizontal to vertical prior to its transfer into the arms of the sub-assembly tool, to the left, where it will be paired with a vacuum-vessel sector, in July 2025.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — Vacuum-vessel sector #3 has been positioned under a gantry crane for an outdoor “flip” operation that will expose its second side, in May 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — The cryostat base hangs above the opening of the 30-meter-deep tokamak assembly pit, before being slowly lowered to the bottom. When complete, the ITER cryostat will become the largest stainless-steel high-vacuum pressure chamber ever built, containing the vacuum vessel and its powerful magnets.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — A view of one of the vacuum-vessel sectors lying on its side inside the former Cryostat Workshop, which is now used for assembly-related activities

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — The fourth and last vacuum-vessel sector from Korea is ready to leave the staging area and travel the last leg of its journey to France from the Hyundai Heavy Industries shipyard in Ulsan, South Korea, in 2024. Each of the 440-ton components was packed in metal cabin-like shipping containers for the long ocean journey.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
Science Photo Library / Reuters — Construction is underway inside the liquid nitrogen plant, part of ITER’s massive cryoplant, which will produce and circulate liquid nitrogen and liquid helium to the shielding and electromagnets of the tokamak.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — The first 110-ton module of the central solenoid, seen from below on December 6, 2023. When complete, the central solenoid will form an 18-meter-tall column at the heart of the tokamak, acting as an incredibly powerful superconducting magnet, driving current into the plasma. Teams from the United States fabricated the central solenoid, then shipped the modules to France. Thirty-five nations around the world are collaborating on the ITER project, building and contributing “in-kind” components and sharing the costs of construction and operation.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — A snapshot of construction progress on the assembly building and the Tokamak Complex, seen on April 7, 2025

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization / EJF Riche — An aerial view of the 100-acre central platform of the ITER Complex, seen in early 2026 in Saint-Paul-lès-Durance, France. The largest buildings, seen at the center, are the Tokamak Complex and its assembly building. Surrounding it are more than 35 support buildings that house workshops, diagnostic centers, heating, cooling, power handling, and more. Construction continues, with a target date of 2033 for its first successful generation of plasma.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
Science Photo Library / Reuters — The 300,000-ton tokamak complex will rest on top of 493 anti-seismic bearings that are mounted on concrete plinths rising up from the foundation.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
Science Photo Library / Reuters — Reinforcing bars are laid in place across the facility’s foundation, before a 1.5-meter-thick concrete foundation slab will be poured over. The building will house the tokamak of the ITER experimental fusion reactor. The acronym ITER is also a word in Latin, meaning “journey,” or “path.”

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Building the World’s Largest Fusion Reactor
item.description:
© ITER Organization — Four massive vacuum-vessel modules sit in place around the central solenoid coil of the ITER tokamak (a type of fusion reactor) in April 2026. Each of the nine wedge-shaped modules stands 13 meters (42.65 feet) tall and weighs approximately 1,200 tons. They are designed to contain a huge volume of intensely-hot plasma inside a torus-shaped vacuum vessel, confined by powerful magnetic fields.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

Wilmington junior Julian Martins tries to cut down a runner during an 11-2 home-opening loss to Winchester on April 6, 2026. Conor Kinneen/Wilmington High School
